1.11a: Contact details (address, fax, email, phone) are provided on the letter of instruction.
Contact via LMS portal for pending property purchases.
Contact by email for all other queries.
Offset Flexible Mortgage: Contact the firm specified in the letter of instruction.
1.11b: Contact procedures are identical to section 1.11a.
1.7: The lender will not lend if the borrower and mortgagor are not the same entity.
1.14: Firm may not act if the conveyancer or an immediate family member is the seller.
Firm may act if a separate fee earner or partner within the firm acts for the lender.
1.15: Firm may not act if the conveyancer or an immediate family member is the borrower.
Firm may act if a separate fee earner or partner within the firm acts for the lender.
3.1.3: Do not provide name/address of the seller's firm unless explicitly requested.
3.1.4: Notify the lender via contact points in 1.11a if the seller lacks legal representation for a decision on proceeding.
3.1.5: Acceptable identity documents: Old style UK driving licence, HMRC Tax notification, Construction Industry tax exemption certificate.
3.2.3: Do not advise of the firm acting for the seller unless specifically requested.
3.2.4: Notify the lender as per section 1.11a if the seller is unrepresented.
4.5a: Standard cases: Contact Mortgage Centre via 1.11a.
Offset Flexible Mortgage: NatWest Offset Completions Team, Norwich (0345 300 4012, Fax 0845 366 0498).
4.5b: LMS instructions: Submit Certificate of Title through the LMS portal.
Other cases: Send to Mortgage Centre as per 1.11.
Offset Flexible Mortgage: Send to NatWest Offset Completions Team, Norwich.
4.1: Valuation report provided with mortgage offer if internal inspection was performed.
No valuation report provided for non-physical inspections; borrower must provide property info.
Shared Ownership: Conveyancer must request the valuation report and verify for discrepancies.
4.3: Report incorrect valuer assumptions via contact point 1.11a.
5.1.1: If seller owned property < 6 months, report: seller's name/address, prior purchase price, connections between parties, and details of any work performed.
5.2.1: Report cases where seller is not the registered owner via 1.11a.
5.4.4: Environmental/contaminated land reports should not be sent to the lender; provide to borrower only.
Notify lender if property is within 300 meters of HS2.
Notify lender of mining reports only if entry is less than 20 meters.
Insurance must be available on normal terms.
5.4.5: Personal searches accepted if the agent has adequate professional indemnity insurance.
5.4.6: Search insurance accepted if it protects the lender and title is good and marketable.
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission is not required.
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents is not required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required.
5.5.4: Report use restrictions affecting value/marketability via 1.11a.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds accepted if positive covenants are enforceable.
Maximum Loan to Value (LTV) restricted to 90%.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats accepted if positive covenants are enforceable.
Maximum Loan to Value (LTV) restricted to 90%.
5.7.1c: Report accepted flying freeholds or freehold flats via 1.11a.
5.8.1: Security on converted buildings with up to four flats is accepted if the borrower occupies one and owns the freehold.
5.8.5: Accepted if security includes both the leasehold flat to be occupied and the freehold reversion of the other flat.
5.9.1: The lender does not lend on commonhold property.
5.13.1: Deposits/gifts from parents/guardians are accepted; second charges in their favor are permitted.
Foreign nationals without permanent UK residency must provide deposit from own resources.
Non-seller third-party gifts accepted only if non-repayable and no second charge is registered.
Seller gifts are treated as incentives affecting purchase price/loan amount.
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords via 1.11a.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is accepted for absent or insolvent landlords.
5.14.17b: Disregard building safety instructions if building < 11m, < 5 storeys, or has satisfactory EWS1.
Notify lender if remediation work assumptions are incorrect or if owner is a non-qualifying leaseholder.
5.14.1: Minimum lease term: Mortgage term plus 30 years.
Shared Ownership: Minimum 75 years plus mortgage term at outset.
5.14.9: Ground rent provisions potentially creating an AST (Housing Act 1988) are acceptable if lease is varied or indemnity insurance is used.
Ground rents exceeding RPI or doubling more frequently than every 25 years are unacceptable.
Leasehold Reform Act 2022 compliance required for new leases: ground rent must be a peppercorn.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms.
5.14.12: Receipts not required for standard mortgages.
Offset Flexible Mortgage: Service charge/ground rent receipts required after completion.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service not required.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificate not required.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer form not required.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association not required.
5.17.5a: Certified copy of Power of Attorney must be sent after completion.
5.17.5b: Certified copy of statutory declaration of non-revocation of Power of Attorney must be sent after completion.
5.19.1: Lends on Shared Ownership if lease contains fundamental clauses for Alienation, Mortgagee Protection, Rent Review, and Staircasing.
Compliant with AHP 2021-2026 or SOAHP 2016-2021 model leases.
5.20.1: Report solar PV panel leases that fail UK Finance minimum requirements via 1.11a.
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed via 1.11a.
6.4.4: Report builder incentives on new builds if they total more than 5% of purchase price.
Do not send the UK Finance Disclosure Form.
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements not required for standard ASTs.
Non-assured tenancies require: max 3-year term, break clause for lender, no business use, and recognition of mortgagee's rights.
6.6.4: The lender does not lend on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO).
6.7.1: Acceptable warranties: NHBC, Zurich, BLP, Build Zone, LABC, Premier (houses only), Global Home, The Q Policy, BOPAS, ABC+, etc.
Refer Premier Guarantee for flats to the lender.
6.7.4: Professional Consultant’s Certificate accepted in lieu of warranty if monitoring occurred during construction and issued prior to exchange.
6.8.1: Report unadopted roads/sewers via 1.11a; include adoption proposals, maintenance costs, and access rights.
6.12.1: Lender does not lend on properties subject to overage agreements.
6.13.1: Report improvement/repair grants: specify repayment terms, amount, and priority relative to mortgage.
7.3: Consent required from all occupants 17+ years old, excluding borrower's children under 26.
Lifetime Mortgage: Spouses/civil partners must be joint owners and mortgagors.
8.1: Conveyancer may not advise third parties.
Must recommend independent legal advice for guarantors and occupiers providing consent.
9.1: Do not send indemnity policy documents; details must be provided in the Certificate of Title.
9.2: Indemnity insurance limit must equal the property value.
10.2b: Minimum notice for mortgage advance: 7 working days.
10.7: Delayed completion: Return funds via CHAPS within one working day.
Offset: Return to NatWest Offset Completions Team, Norwich.
14.1.4: CH2 application required if Offer refers to a Reserve Facility.
14.1.5: Do not send original mortgage deed; retain on file or send to Land Registry.
Update LMS every 30 days if registration is not completed within 30 days of completion.
14.2.2: LMS cases: Upload Priority Notice, AP1, Official Copy, and (if applicable) Power of Attorney, Guarantee, and Occupier Consent.
Non-LMS cases: Only send Deed of Consent/Postponement or Power of Attorney docs to 1.11a.
16.3.1: Standard transfer/deed of covenant not used; reconstitution of security required for Transfer of Equity.
16.4.2: Copy of proposed tenancy required unless it is a standard AST.
17.1.1: Personal Mortgages: Redemption requests via LMS Secure Link only.
Offset Mortgages: CMSRedemptionsTeam@natwest.com or written request to Norwich office.
17.2.1a: Lender arranges discharge via Land Registry after redemption; no written confirmation provided to conveyancer.
